answers: Factory farming is industrial-scale animal agriculture that prioritizes maximum production at minimum cost. Animals are kept in confined, crowded conditions to maximize output.
Factory farming holo industrial bhabe onek prani ke choto jayga te rekhe mass production kora. Chicken, pig, cow — shob confined space e rakha hoy.
Factory farming raises serious concerns: animal welfare issues, antibiotic overuse leading to superbugs, environmental pollution, worker exploitation, and contribution to climate change.

Over 70% of farm animals globally are raised in factory farms. Alternatives include free-range farming, organic practices, and reducing meat consumption to lower demand for industrial animal agriculture.
